name: Pittsburgh Land Bank
description: Acquires, manages, and transfers vacant, abandoned, and tax-delinquent properties in Pittsburgh for productive reuse and redevelopment. Formerly known as the URA Land Reserve.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, second Friday of each month at 1:00 PM. No meeting in January 2025. 2026 schedule not yet posted as of March 2026.
meeting_location: Hybrid: Zoom Webinar (registration required) and Pittsburgh City Cable Channel broadcast. Meetings are not held in a fixed in-person location — Zoom is the primary format.
livestream_url: https://www.youtube.com/PittsburghCityChannel (City's YouTube Channel); also televised on Pittsburgh City Cable Channel
public_comment_details: Public comment is accepted at the beginning of each meeting (immediately after roll call) and again at the end of the meeting. Members may register to give live comment via the form at pghlandbank.org or submit written comment to staff@pghlandbank.org. Registration closes at 11:00 AM the day of the meeting.
recent_activity: In March 2026, the Pittsburgh City Council (Standing Committees, March 11, 2026) affirmatively recommended Resolution 2026-0207 authorizing the Pittsburgh Land Bank to acquire City right, title, and interest in property at 635 Kirkpatrick Street (Block 10-K, Lot 152) in the 5th Ward.

upcoming_meetings.notes: Second Friday of March 2026 — registration required via pghlandbank.org
Second Friday of April 2026 — estimated based on pattern
Second Friday of May 2026 — estimated based on pattern

name: Pittsburgh Art Commission (Public Art & Civic Design Commission — PACD)
description: The Public Art & Civic Design Commission (PACD) — the current formal name for the Pittsburgh Art Commission — empowers development of art and culture in public spaces and reviews aesthetics of the designed environment on or over City-owned property. It operates through two committees: the Public Art Review Committee and the Civic Design Review Committee.
meeting_schedule: Fourth Wednesday of each month at 2:00 PM (December recess — no meeting in December).
meeting_location: In-person and virtual (hybrid). In-person: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Boulevard of the Allies, Pittsburgh, PA 15219. Virtual via Zoom: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85414433221 (Webinar ID: 854 1443 3221; phone: +1 301 715 8592).
public_comment_details: Public comment is accepted during Hearing and Action items. Attendees may comment in-person or via Zoom (raise hand function). Email pacd@pittsburghpa.gov to receive agendas.
recent_activity: February 25, 2026: PACD approved application PACD-2025-15 for a Percent for Art public art project in the Hill District at 3002 Center Avenue, submitted by artists Alicia Young and Naomi Chambers. The Civic Design Committee also met that day to review a civic design project. Meeting available on YouTube.

upcoming_meetings.format: Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom
Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom
Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom
upcoming_meetings.notes: Confirmed meeting page exists at pittsburghpa.gov

name: Pittsburgh Historic Review Commission (HRC)
description: Reviews changes to properties in Pittsburgh's historic districts and issues Certificates of Appropriateness. Also hears historic nominations. Part of the Department of City Planning.
meeting_schedule: First Wednesday of each month at 1:00 PM (runs until 5:00 PM). January meeting starts at 12:00 PM.
meeting_location: In-person and virtual (hybrid). In-person: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Boulevard of the Allies, Pittsburgh, PA 15219. Virtual via Zoom: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/88232493998 (Webinar ID: 882 3249 3998; phone: +1 312 626 6799).
public_comment_details: Public testimony accepted for Hearing and Action items. Can be provided in-person, via Zoom (raise hand), by phone (press *9), by email to historicreview@pittsburghpa.gov (by noon the business day before the meeting), or by letter (must arrive 2 business days before the meeting).
recent_activity: February 4, 2026 meeting heard alteration case for 933 Penn Avenue (Penn-Liberty Historic District) and a building alteration/new rear garage at 108 N. Dithridge Street (Oakland Civic Center). January 7, 2026 meeting heard historic district expansion for the Mexican War Streets district and 1229 Recluse Place.

upcoming_meetings.format: Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom
Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom
Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom

name: City of Pittsburgh Equal Opportunity Review Commission (EORC)
description: Reviews and approves applicable professional service and construction contracts for compliance with Chapter 161 of the Pittsburgh Code of Ordinances, and monitors contractor compliance with City policies on minority and women business enterprise (MBE/WBE) opportunities. Also develops policies regarding employment opportunities for minorities and women in City contracting.
meeting_schedule: Monthly at 12:00 PM (noon). 2026 dates: January 15, February 19, March 19, April 16, May 21, June 18, and continuing monthly through the year.
meeting_location: In-person (hybrid with Zoom): City County Building, Room 646, 6th Floor (Learning Lab), 414 Grant Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15219.
livestream_url: Not confirmed. Meetings conducted in hybrid format (Zoom available for remote testimony). Past meetings on Legistar: https://pittsburgh.legistar.com
public_comment_details: At the meeting, the Commission Chair calls for public testimony for each agenda item. In-person testimony at Room 646, 414 Grant Street. Remote testimony via Zoom. Pre-registration by 9:00 AM the day of the meeting requested. EORC Meeting Public Comment Registration Form available on the website. Phone: 412-255-8804.
recent_activity: The City of Pittsburgh Office of Business Diversity hosted an enhanced compliance vendor information session in early 2026 related to EORC. A January 17, 2024 ordinance (2024-0025) amended the EORC's enabling code to clarify scope of review.

upcoming_meetings.format: Hybrid: Room 646, City County Building + Zoom
Hybrid: Room 646, City County Building + Zoom
Hybrid: Room 646, City County Building + Zoom

name: Pittsburgh Civil Service Commission
description: Establishes civil service rules for the City of Pittsburgh, hears appeals of hiring and promotional decisions, and oversees classification and examination procedures for City employees.
meeting_schedule: Every Thursday at 8:00 AM. Meeting dates and times are posted at the Department of Personnel and Civil Service Commission.
meeting_location: Department of Personnel and Civil Service Commission (specific address not publicly listed on website; contact commission for location). No hybrid/virtual format confirmed.
livestream_url: No livestream confirmed. No YouTube channel identified.
public_comment_details: Public meetings and public hearings are held. All public meeting and hearing minutes are public documents available by contacting the Secretary and Chief Examiner at 412-255-2042.
recent_activity: Not available from public sources — minutes must be requested directly from the Commission.

upcoming_meetings.notes: Every Thursday — exact dates estimated based on weekly Thursday schedule

name: Pittsburgh Commission on Human Relations (CHR)
description: Enforces the City of Pittsburgh's anti-discrimination ordinances. Hears discrimination complaints in employment, housing, and public accommodations. Also pursues fair housing enforcement. Operates under the City's Department of Legal Services.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, first Monday of each month at 3:30 PM. (Pattern based on 2025 meeting dates: Jan 6, Feb 3, Mar 3, Apr 7, May 5, Jun 2, Jul 7, Sep 8, Oct 6, Nov 3, Dec 1.)
meeting_location: Hybrid: In-person in Room 902 of the City County Building (take the Ross Street elevators), 414 Grant Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15219. Also held via Zoom.
livestream_url: Livestreamed on YouTube on the City Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@CityChanPGH (City Channel Pittsburgh). Past meetings available on Legistar: https://pittsburgh.legistar.com
public_comment_details: Commission meetings are open to the public. Public comment accepted at each meeting. Meetings begin at 3:30 PM.
recent_activity: December 1, 2025 meeting: Commission heard director's report covering outreach, partnerships, enforcement caseloads, and recent city code updates; scheduled a public hearing for December 10, 2025; approved the 2026 meeting calendar. September 8, 2025 meeting: Discussed 66 pending intake cases, growing caseload from EEOC/PHRC referrals, and 2026 budget requests.

upcoming_meetings.format: Hybrid: Room 902, City County Building + Zoom + YouTube Livestream
Hybrid: Room 902, City County Building + Zoom + YouTube Livestream
Hybrid: Room 902, City County Building + Zoom + YouTube Livestream

name: Pittsburgh Ethics Hearing Board (EHB)
description: Hears cases involving alleged violations of the City of Pittsburgh's Ethics Code. Issues advisory opinions, employment waivers, enforcement orders, settlements, and regulations. Monitors candidates' statements of financial interest and campaign finance disclosures.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, first Thursday of each month at 4:00 PM. Public meeting times are reserved monthly; times may be used for public meetings or for non-public executive sessions. 2025 dates: 1/9, 2/6, 3/4, 4/3, 5/1, 6/5, 7/10, 8/7, 9/4, 10/2, 11/6, 12/4. 2026 dates confirmed include: 3/5, 5/7 (from City Planning Calendar which also shows Ethics Hearing Board listings), 11/5, 12/3.
meeting_location: Conference room 902, City County Building, 414 Grant Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15219 (use Ross Street elevators). Also conducted via video conference.
livestream_url: Meetings available on City's Legistar site. No dedicated YouTube livestream confirmed. Past meetings: https://pittsburgh.legistar.com
public_comment_details: Meetings are open to the public. The Ethics Hearing Board meets as needed for public meetings or executive sessions. Contact the Ethics Office for details on participating.
recent_activity: Not available from public sources as of March 2026. Ethics Board handles confidential advisory opinions and enforcement matters. Campaign finance disclosures and statements of financial interest are posted on the city website.

upcoming_meetings.format: Hybrid: Conference Room 902, City County Building + Video conference
Hybrid: Conference Room 902, City County Building + Video conference
Hybrid: Conference Room 902, City County Building + Video conference

name: Pittsburgh Comprehensive Municipal Pension Trust Fund Board (CMPTF)
description: Oversees the City of Pittsburgh's three pension funds: the Municipal Pension Fund, the Firemen's Relief and Pension Fund, and the Policemen's Relief and Pension Fund. A 7-member board including the Mayor, City Council President, and employee/retiree representatives.
meeting_schedule: Quarterly. 2026 approved meeting dates: February 5, 2026; May 7, 2026; September 3, 2026; December date TBD (approved at December 4, 2025 board meeting).
meeting_location: Mayor's Conference Room, 5th Floor, City County Building, 414 Grant Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15219 at 1:00 PM.
livestream_url: No livestream confirmed. Not on YouTube.
public_comment_details: No public comment confirmed. The board operates as a fiduciary body. Public notices are posted per Pennsylvania Sunshine Act.
recent_activity: December 4, 2025 board meeting approved the 2026 meeting calendar and fiscal year-end financials. Board members as of 2026 include Mayor Corey O'Connor, Council President R. Daniel Lavelle, and Ralph Sicuro (Chairperson). Former Mayor Ed Gainey served through December 31, 2025.

upcoming_meetings.format: In-person: Mayor's Conference Room, 5th Floor, City County Building
In-person: Mayor's Conference Room, 5th Floor, City County Building

name: Pittsburgh Parking Authority Board (Public Parking Authority of Pittsburgh)
description: Manages public parking garages, surface lots, and parking meters throughout the City of Pittsburgh. Sets parking rates, manages enforcement, and oversees capital projects including garage maintenance.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, third Thursday of each month at 10:00 AM. No meeting in June 2026. March 2026 meeting rescheduled from March 19 to March 26. 2026 schedule runs January through December (11 meetings total).
meeting_location: Virtual via Microsoft Teams (meetings held virtually until further notice). Teams link: https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/27432194258130?p=SUnKYtj6aCKjF7m0ff. Meeting ID: 274 321 942 581 30. Phone: +1 312-754-8784,,945134885#.
livestream_url: No dedicated public livestream confirmed. Meetings held on Microsoft Teams (virtual).
public_comment_details: No public comment confirmed on the website. Meetings are open to the public per the Pennsylvania Sunshine Act.
recent_activity: January 15, 2026 meeting approved minutes from December 18, 2025. November 21, 2025 meeting approved 2026 board meeting dates. September 18, 2025 minutes show discussion of enforcement: approximately 4,200 more tickets issued in August compared to prior month. The authority began automated street sweeping enforcement on April 1, 2025.

upcoming_meetings.notes: Rescheduled from March 19 — notice published in Pittsburgh Post-Gazette

name: Sports & Exhibition Authority of Pittsburgh and Allegheny County (SEA)
description: Manages the David L. Lawrence Convention Center (DLCC), oversees the Sports & Exhibition Fund (SEF) grant program for sports and convention events, and administers North Shore properties. Successor to the Public Auditorium Authority. Joint City of Pittsburgh and Allegheny County body.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, typically the second Thursday of each month at 10:30 AM. Cancelled meetings occur occasionally (e.g., January 2025 and August 2025 were cancelled).
meeting_location: In-person at David L. Lawrence Convention Center (DLCC) Executive Board Room, First Floor, 1000 Fort Duquesne Blvd, Pittsburgh, PA 15222 (specific room may vary — e.g., Room 305, Room 405 have been used in 2025).
livestream_url: No public livestream identified. Minutes and agendas posted at https://www.pgh-sea.com
public_comment_details: Public participation at open meetings is a statutory right. Members of the public may provide comments by email prior to the meeting, by telephone, or via Zoom chat (when applicable). Public participation is the first and last item on the agenda.
recent_activity: 2025 SEF grant activity included awards to sports events at multiple Allegheny County venues. Notable 2025 grants: $35,000 to Alpha Tournament Company for youth hockey (Oct 2025–Feb 2026), $60,000 to PA Interscholastic Esports Association for an October 2025 event at UPMC Events Center, $30,000 to Pittsburgh Riverhounds (Friends of Pittsburgh Professional Soccer) for soccer tournaments. The SEA manages DLCC operational contracts and North Shore parking assets.

upcoming_meetings.notes: Second Thursday of March 2026 — estimated based on pattern; confirm at pgh-sea.com

name: Stadium Authority of the City of Pittsburgh
description: Five-member board appointed by the Mayor that holds the City's interests in Acrisure Stadium (Pittsburgh Steelers) and PNC Park (Pittsburgh Pirates). Oversees the lease agreements, capital projects, and physical maintenance of the stadiums. The SEA provides operational staffing to the Stadium Authority.
meeting_schedule: Quarterly (approximately 4 times per year). Confirmed 2026 meeting dates: March 26, 2026; June 10, 2026; September 17, 2026; December date TBD.
meeting_location: David L. Lawrence Convention Center, Executive Board Room, First Floor, 1000 Fort Duquesne Blvd, Pittsburgh, PA 15222 at 10:00 AM.
livestream_url: No public livestream confirmed. Meeting changes advertised in a newspaper of general circulation at least 24 hours prior.
public_comment_details: Public participation at open meetings is a statutory right accorded to residents and taxpayers of Allegheny County. Contact 412-393-0200 for minutes or questions.
recent_activity: December 10, 2025: Stadium Authority quarterly meeting approved parking fee increases for Pitt football games at Acrisure Stadium, with Alco Parking Corp. receiving rate increases. The meeting also set the 2026 board meeting calendar. A 2026 Stadium Authority agreement (2026-0202) was recommended by Pittsburgh City Council's Standing Committees on March 11, 2026.

upcoming_meetings.notes: Confirmed — notice posted at pgh-sea.com and Pittsburgh Post-Gazette classifieds

name: Pittsburgh-Allegheny County Task Force on Disabilities (City-County Task Force on Disability — CCTFD)
description: A 13-member panel of advocates, service providers, and consumers appointed by the Mayor and County Executive. Advises the City and County on issues affecting people with disabilities in the region. Also operates a subcommittee: the Disability Advocacy and Inclusion Committee (DAIC).
meeting_schedule: Monthly, third Monday of each month at 2:00 PM (except holidays). 2026 confirmed CCTFD meeting dates: March 16 (Virtual), April 20 (Virtual), May 18 (Virtual), June 15, July 20, August 17, September 28, October 19, November 16, December 21.
meeting_location: Virtual only through at least May 2026 (Zoom). In-person meetings resume June 2026 onward. Join virtual meeting link available at pittsburghpa.gov/Resident-Services/ADA-Disability-Rights/City-County-Task-Force-on-Disability.
livestream_url: No public livestream confirmed. Past meetings available on Legistar: https://pittsburgh.legistar.com
public_comment_details: Meetings are open to the public. Subscribe to meeting notifications via CCTFDNews listserv. Contact: ada@pittsburghpa.gov.
recent_activity: 2026 meeting calendar published in March 2026. The Disability Advocacy and Inclusion Committee (DAIC) meets separately on the second Monday of each month at 2:00 PM via Zoom (2026 dates: March 9, April 13, May 11, June 8, July 13, August 10, September 14, October 12, November 9, December 14).

name: Pittsburgh Citizens Police Review Board (CPRB)
description: An independent civilian oversight board that reviews complaints against Pittsburgh Bureau of Police officers, conducts its own investigations, and makes findings and recommendations to the Chief of Police and Mayor. Separate from the City's Office of Municipal Investigations.
meeting_schedule: Monthly, last Tuesday of each month at 6:00 PM. 2026 confirmed dates: February 24; March 24; April 28; May 26 (from 2026 proposed calendar). January 2026 was cancelled. No meeting in August (summer recess).
meeting_location: Virtual via Zoom. Zoom join link: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/88418944985. Phone: +1 305 224 1968 or +1 309 205 3325. Meetings have been held via Zoom since the COVID-19 pandemic.
livestream_url: Livestreamed simultaneously on CPRB's YouTube Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@cprbpgh/streams. Full video archive: https://cprbpgh.org/for-public-record/video
public_comment_details: Meetings are open to the public. All meetings start at 6:00 PM. Public may attend via Zoom or watch the YouTube livestream.
recent_activity: December 2, 2025 meeting approved minutes and case agenda. January 27, 2026 meeting agenda was posted, but meeting was cancelled. February 24, 2026 meeting proceeded with case review agenda and draft minutes from December 2025. 2026 meeting calendar was proposed in January 2026.
